overview: A local-first web app that lets Royal Caribbean content authors search, browse, and upload digital assets from the AEM DAM export. A React frontend communicates with an Express backend backed by SQLite. Assets are ingested from existing .content.xml metadata files, enriched with AI vision (OpenAI GPT-4o) on upload, and made searchable via natural language full-text search and structured filters.
goals: Ingest all 49 existing DAM assets (XML metadata + thumbnails) into SQLite on startup
Provide natural language full-text search across all metadata fields
Support structured filters: category, subcategory, rights status, location, metadata quality flags
Show asset detail popup with original metadata vs AI-enriched metadata side by side
Allow upload of single images or folders — auto-enrich with OpenAI Vision on upload
Highlight metadata quality issues (missing fields, rights problems, sync errors, release placeholders)
nonGoals: No user authentication or session management
No editing or updating of existing .content.xml files
No Celebrity Cruises assets in scope
No bulk export or download features
successMetrics: All 49 existing assets appear in the browse grid on first load
Natural language query returns relevant results within 200ms
Uploaded image is enriched and searchable within 10 seconds
Asset detail modal shows original and enriched fields side by side
All quality gates pass: npm test, npm run lint, npm run build
openQuestions: Should uploaded assets be stored inside ../Data/royal/ to match existing structure, or in uploads/ directory?
Should AI enrichment call happen synchronously on upload (blocking response) or asynchronously (polling)?
What OpenAI model version — gpt-4o or gpt-4o-mini for cost efficiency?

stack.ai: OpenAI GPT-4o Vision API (OPENAI_API_KEY env var)

layout: client/src/api/             Fetch wrapper for Express API
package.json                Root workspace with dev/test/lint/build scripts

routes.name: Health Check
routes.purpose: Returns { status: 'ok' } — used to verify server is running

routes.name: Search Assets
routes.purpose: Full-text + structured filter search. Query params: q, category, subcategory, rights_status, location, has_title, has_rights, has_release_placeholder, limit, offset

routes.name: Asset Detail
routes.purpose: Return single asset with all original and enriched metadata fields plus quality_issues array and cdn_url

routes.name: Filter Options
routes.purpose: Return all distinct filter values: categories, subcategories, locations, rights_statuses, and counts

routes.name: Upload Asset(s)
routes.purpose: Accept multipart/form-data with one or more image files. Save to uploads/, insert rows to DB, trigger AI enrichment per file, return enriched asset records

routes.name: Re-enrich Asset
routes.purpose: Trigger AI Vision enrichment for an existing asset by ID. Updates enriched_* fields and FTS index.

uiNotes: Brand colors: Navy #001B6B (primary), Gold #C8960C (accent), white backgrounds
Header: 'Royal Caribbean' wordmark in navy, 'DAM Asset Intelligence' subtitle in gold, Upload button on right
Main layout: left filter sidebar (260px fixed) + right asset grid (fluid)
Asset grid: responsive CSS grid, min 200px cards, 16:9 thumbnail aspect ratio with object-fit cover
Asset card shows: thumbnail (grey placeholder if missing), title (filename fallback), category badge, rights dot (green=owned, amber=unclear, red=none), quality warning icon if any issues
Asset detail: full-screen modal overlay, close on X or ESC, left = large image + technical info, right = two-column metadata table (Original | Enriched)
Enriched column values in navy text; cells with no enriched value show grey dash; enriched values differing from original get a gold left border
Quality Issues section in modal: each issue shown as a badge — 'Missing Rights', 'Release Placeholder', 'S7 Sync Error', etc.
CDN URL shown in monospace code block with Copy button — shows 'Copied!' for 2s on click
'Enrich with AI' button in modal when asset has no enriched data
Upload: modal with drag-and-drop zone, multiple file support, folder upload via webkitdirectory toggle
Upload modal shows per-file status: pending → uploading → enriching → done / error
Search bar: prominent, debounced 300ms, natural language placeholder
Active filters shown as removable chips below search bar
Empty state: friendly message with 'Clear filters' action

dataModel.description: Main table — original XML metadata + deterministic enrichment + AI enrichment
Full-text search index synced from assets table

uploadFlow.description: Three-stage pipeline triggered when a user uploads files

stages.name: File Storage
stages.detail: multer receives multipart/form-data, validates MIME type (jpeg/png/webp) and size (<20MB), saves each file to uploads/<uuid>-<sanitized-name>, inserts a minimal asset row with enrichment_source='pending'

stages.name: AI Vision Enrichment
stages.where: Express — POST /api/assets/:id/enrich (called per file after upload)
stages.detail: Reads the saved file from disk, base64-encodes it, sends to OpenAI GPT-4o Vision with a structured prompt requesting JSON: { title, description, tags[], location, scene, subjects[], mood, channel_hint, confidence }. Writes all returned fields to enriched_* columns. Updates FTS index.

stages.name: Frontend Status Update
stages.detail: After each enrich call resolves, file status changes to 'done'. When all files finish, a 'View assets' button refreshes the asset grid. If OPENAI_API_KEY is missing, enrichment returns 503 — file status shows 'Uploaded, enrichment unavailable' but the asset is still stored and accessible.

rules: Display title priority: enriched_title → original_title → filename (without extension)
Display tags: merge enriched_tags + original_tags arrays, deduplicate, sort
Rights status: 'owned' if rights_owner contains 'royal caribbean' (case-insensitive, trimmed); 'unclear' if rights data present but has typo or inconsistency; 'none' if no rights data in either field

Channel inference (deterministic, from filename + dimensions): filename contains 'hero' → hero; 'banner' → banner; 'mobile' or portrait (height > width) → mobile; 'desktop' or landscape (width > height) → desktop
Creator normalization: map '[Royal Carribean]', 'Brand Team', 'Brand Team ', '[Brand Team]', '[Production Team ]' → 'Royal Caribbean Brand Team'
Release placeholder: if any metadata field contains the string 'Selecione' → set has_release_placeholder = 1
FTS sync: after any INSERT or UPDATE on assets, sync the corresponding row in assets_fts
On server startup: run DB migrations, then run XML ingest if SELECT COUNT(*) FROM assets = 0
DATA_DIR env var points to the existing DAM export: default '../Data/royal' (relative to server/)
Uploaded files stored in uploads/ directory at project root (relative to where ralph runs)

stories.title: Scaffold project structure
stories.description: As a developer, I want a working monorepo with Express backend and React Vite frontend so that I can build and test both apps from a single root.
acceptanceCriteria: Root package.json (at project root where ralph runs) has workspaces: ['client', 'server']
server/ is an Express app — install: npm install express better-sqlite3 multer cors dotenv openai uuid
server/ has scripts: start (node src/index.js), dev (nodemon src/index.js), test (jest --runInBand), lint (eslint src)
client/ is a React + Vite app created with: npm create vite@latest client -- --template react
client/ installs: npm install axios
client/ has scripts: dev (vite), build (vite build), test (vitest), lint (eslint src)
Root package.json has scripts: dev (concurrently runs server:dev and client:dev), test (runs server tests then client tests), lint (runs both linters), build (runs client build)
npm install concurrently at root
server/src/index.js starts Express on port 3001, registers CORS for localhost:5173, has GET /api/health → { status: 'ok' }

client/src/App.jsx renders

Royal Caribbean DAM
.env.example at project root: OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key_here, PORT=3001, DATA_DIR=../Data/royal
.gitignore includes: node_modules/, dam.sqlite, uploads/, .env
uploads/ directory created with a .gitkeep file
Example: npm run dev starts both servers — browser shows 'Royal Caribbean DAM', curl localhost:3001/api/health returns {status:'ok'}
Negative case: missing OPENAI_API_KEY does not crash server startup — logs a warning 'AI enrichment disabled: OPENAI_API_KEY not set'

stories.title: Database schema and migrations
stories.description: As a developer, I want SQLite tables created automatically on server startup so that the app has persistent storage without manual setup.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/db/schema.js exports runMigrations(db) function
runMigrations creates the assets table with all fields defined in the data model using CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS

runMigrations creates the assets_fts FTS5 virtual table: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS assets_fts USING fts5(id, title, description, tags, location, creator, category, subcategory, filename, content='assets', content_rowid='rowid')
runMigrations creates INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggers on assets to keep assets_fts in sync automatically
server/src/db/index.js opens dam.sqlite at project root, calls runMigrations, exports the db instance
server/src/index.js imports db from db/index.js on startup
Jest test: runMigrations on an in-memory SQLite DB (:memory:) creates assets and assets_fts without error
Jest test: insert a row into assets → assets_fts trigger fires → SELECT from assets_fts returns that row
Jest test: runMigrations called twice does not throw or duplicate tables
Negative case: if dam.sqlite cannot be created (bad path), server logs the error and exits with process.exit(1)

stories.title: XML ingest service
stories.description: As a developer, I want all existing .content.xml asset files parsed and loaded into SQLite on startup so that the 49 DAM assets are immediately available without manual import.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/services/ingest.js exports ingestAssets(db, dataDir)
Recursively walks dataDir, finds .content.xml files where the parent folder name ends in .jpg/.jpeg/.png/.JPG and the path does not contain _jcr_content

Parses all fields: dc:title, dc:description, dc:creator, dc:rights, xmpRights:Owner, xmpRights:UsageTerms, Iptc4xmpExt:LocationShown, tiff:ImageWidth, tiff:ImageLength, dam:size, dam:Fileformat, dam:MIMEtype, dam:scene7File, dam:scene7Domain, dam:scene7FileStatus, dam:lastS7SyncStatus (on jcr:content), cq:parentPath, cq:name, cq:tags
Strips JCR type wrappers: '{Long}1920' → 1920, '{Boolean}true' → true, '{Date}...' → string
Infers category/subcategory/subsubcategory from path segments relative to dataDir

Applies deterministic enrichment rules: creator normalization, rights reconciliation into enriched_rights_status, channel/format from filename+dimensions, sets has_release_placeholder=1 if any field contains 'Selecione', sets s7_sync_error=1 if lastS7SyncStatus starts with 'failed'
Sets thumbnail_path to relative path of cq5dam.thumbnail.319.319.png if file exists on disk
Sets web_image_path to relative path of cq5dam.web.1280.1280.jpeg if file exists on disk
Uses INSERT OR IGNORE so re-running ingest is safe
server/src/index.js calls ingestAssets(db, DATA_DIR) on startup if asset count is 0
Jest test: ingestAssets against the real ../Data/royal directory inserts exactly 49 rows
Jest test: asset 'anthem-of-the-seas-new-york-statue-liberty.jpg' has original_title 'Anthem of the Seas Sightseeing the Statue of Liberty, New York'
Jest test: creator normalization — '[Royal Carribean]' → enriched_creator_normalized = 'Royal Caribbean Brand Team'
Jest test: 7 assets have has_release_placeholder = 1
Jest test: 6 assets have s7_sync_error = 1
Jest test: 27 assets have enriched_rights_status = 'none'
Negative case: malformed XML file is skipped with console.warn — ingest completes for remaining files

stories.title: GET /api/assets — search and filter endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want to search and filter assets via the API so that I can find relevant images for my campaign.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/assets returns { total, assets: [] } with all 49 assets and 200 status when no params
Query param q: runs FTS5 search across title, description, tags, location, creator, category, subcategory, filename — returns ranked results
Query param category: exact match filter on category column
Query param subcategory: exact match filter on subcategory column
Query param rights_status: filters by enriched_rights_status — values: owned, unclear, none
Query param location: exact match on enriched_location or original_location
Query param has_title=true returns only assets with non-null/non-empty original_title or enriched_title
Query param has_rights=true returns only assets with any rights data
Query param has_release_placeholder=true returns only the 7 flagged assets
Query params limit (default 100, max 200) and offset (default 0) for pagination

Each asset in response includes: id, filename, category, subcategory, display_title, display_description, display_tags, thumbnail_path, web_image_path, enriched_rights_status, enrichment_source, width, height, scene7_file, scene7_domain, s7_sync_error, needs_rights_review, has_release_placeholder
display_title is: enriched_title ?? original_title ?? filename without extension
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?q=sunset returns at least 1 result containing 'sunset' in title or description
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?category=ships returns only assets with category='ships'
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?rights_status=none returns exactly 27 assets
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?limit=5&offset=0 returns assets array of length 5 and total=49
Negative case: GET /api/assets?q=zzznomatch returns { total: 0, assets: [] } with 200 status

stories.title: GET /api/assets/:id — asset detail endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want full metadata for a single asset including quality issues and CDN URL so that I can review and use the asset.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/assets/:id returns full asset row with all original_* and enriched_* fields and 200 status
Response includes cdn_url: scene7_domain + 'is/image/' + scene7_file (null if either is missing)

Response includes quality_issues array — each issue is a string key: 'missing_title', 'missing_description', 'missing_rights', 'missing_location', 'title_equals_description', 's7_sync_error', 'release_placeholder'
Response includes display_title, display_description, display_tags computed fields
Supertest test: GET /api/assets/anthem-of-the-seas-new-york-statue-liberty.jpg returns correct original_title
Supertest test: an asset with no rights data has 'missing_rights' in quality_issues
Supertest test: an asset with has_release_placeholder=1 has 'release_placeholder' in quality_issues
Negative case: GET /api/assets/does-not-exist returns 404 { error: 'Asset not found' }

stories.title: GET /api/filters — filter options endpoint
stories.description: As a frontend developer, I want a filters endpoint so that the UI can populate filter options dynamically.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/filters returns 200 with { categories, subcategories, locations, rights_statuses, counts }
categories: sorted array of distinct category values
subcategories: object keyed by category, each value is sorted array of subcategory strings
locations: sorted array of distinct non-null location values
rights_statuses: array of { value, count } for owned/unclear/none
counts: { total, missing_title, missing_description, missing_rights, missing_location, has_release_placeholder, s7_sync_error }
Supertest test: categories includes 'ships' and 'promotions'
Supertest test: subcategories.ships includes 'allure', 'anthem', 'grandeur'
Supertest test: counts.has_release_placeholder equals 7
Negative case: empty database returns all arrays empty and all counts 0

stories.title: POST /api/assets/upload — file upload endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want to upload image files via the API so they are stored on disk and registered in the database ready for enrichment.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/assets/upload accepts multipart/form-data with field name 'files' (supports multiple files via multer array)
Each file is saved to uploads/<uuid>-<sanitized-originalname> relative to project root
File validation: MIME type must be image/jpeg, image/png, or image/webp — reject others with 400 { error: 'Unsupported file type. Accepted: jpeg, png, webp' }
File size limit: 20MB per file — multer configured with limits.fileSize = 20 * 1024 * 1024, return 413 if exceeded
For each valid file, insert asset row: id=uuid, filename=originalname, filepath=saved path, category='uploaded', enrichment_source='pending', mime_type, file_size, created_at
Response: 201 { uploaded: N, assets: [{ id, filename, filepath }] }
Supertest test: POST with a valid JPEG returns 201 and asset id
Supertest test: uploaded asset is retrievable via GET /api/assets/:id
Supertest test: file exists on disk at the filepath in the response
Negative case: POST with a .txt file returns 400 with correct error message
Negative case: POST with no files returns 400 { error: 'No files provided' }

stories.title: POST /api/assets/:id/enrich — AI Vision enrichment endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want uploaded images enriched with AI-generated metadata so that they are immediately descriptive and searchable.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/assets/:id/enrich reads asset filepath from DB, reads file from disk as base64
Calls OpenAI chat.completions.create with model gpt-4o, messages including the base64 image
System prompt instructs model to respond with valid JSON only: { title, description, tags, location, scene, subjects, mood, channel_hint, confidence }

On success: updates enriched_title, enriched_description, enriched_tags (JSON.stringify array), enriched_location, enriched_scene, enriched_subjects (JSON.stringify array), enriched_mood, enriched_channel (from channel_hint), enrichment_source='ai-vision', enrichment_confidence, updated_at
FTS trigger automatically updates assets_fts after the UPDATE
Response: 200 with the full updated asset record including all enriched_* fields
If OPENAI_API_KEY env var is not set: return 503 { error: 'AI enrichment unavailable — OPENAI_API_KEY not configured' }
Jest test (mocked OpenAI): enrichment service maps API response fields correctly to DB columns
Jest test (mocked OpenAI): after enrichment, GET /api/assets/:id returns non-null enriched_title
Jest test (mocked OpenAI): after enrichment, asset appears in FTS search by its enriched_title
Negative case: OpenAI API returns error → return 502 { error: 'Enrichment failed', detail: message } — asset row unchanged
Negative case: asset has no filepath (XML-ingested without local image) → return 400 { error: 'No image file available for enrichment' }
Negative case: filepath in DB does not exist on disk → return 404 { error: 'Image file not found on disk' }

stories.title: React app shell, layout, and API client
stories.description: As a developer, I want a branded React app shell with a consistent layout and a typed API client so all pages share structure and API calls are centralised.
acceptanceCriteria: App.jsx renders <Header /> above a <main> content area
Header component: left = 'Royal Caribbean' in navy bold + 'DAM Asset Intelligence' subtitle in gold; right = 'Upload' button in gold
Header is fixed/sticky at top, 64px tall, navy background, gold bottom border
client/src/api/assets.js exports: getAssets(params), getAsset(id), getFilters(), uploadFiles(formData), enrichAsset(id)
All API functions use axios with baseURL http://localhost:3001
getAssets accepts an object of query params and serialises them as query string
Vitest test: Header renders text 'Royal Caribbean'
Vitest test: Header renders a button with text 'Upload'
Vitest test: getAssets called with { category: 'ships' } makes GET request to /api/assets?category=ships
Negative case: axios network error is thrown as-is (not swallowed) — callers handle the error

stories.title: Search and browse page
stories.description: As a content author, I want to search and browse assets on the main page with filters so that I can find the right image quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Route / renders the search/browse page with SearchBar, FilterSidebar, and AssetGrid
SearchBar: text input, placeholder 'Search assets by keyword, location, mood...', onChange debounced 300ms, updates q param in search state

FilterSidebar (260px): Category section with chip toggles (ships / promotions), Subcategory section with dynamic chips from getFilters(), Rights Status (owned / unclear / none chips), Location dropdown, Metadata Quality toggles (Missing Title, Missing Rights, Release Placeholder)
All filter state combined and passed to getAssets on change
Active filters displayed as removable chips below the search bar
AssetGrid: CSS grid, repeat(auto-fill, minmax(200px, 1fr)), gap 16px
AssetCard: 16:9 thumbnail (object-fit cover), grey placeholder if no thumbnail, display_title (2-line clamp), category badge, rights dot, warning icon if quality_issues non-empty
Results count label above grid: 'Showing X of Y assets'
Loading state: show 12 skeleton cards (grey animated placeholder) while request in-flight
Empty state: 'No assets match your search.' with a 'Clear all filters' button
Clicking a card opens the AssetDetailModal passing the asset id
Vitest test: renders correct number of cards matching mocked API response
Vitest test: typing 'sunset' in SearchBar triggers getAssets with q='sunset' after debounce
Vitest test: clicking category chip 'ships' calls getAssets with category='ships'
Vitest test: clicking a chip's remove (×) button calls getAssets without that filter
Negative case: API returns error → error banner shown above grid, grid is empty, no crash

stories.title: Asset detail modal
stories.description: As a content author, I want a full-detail popup for any asset so I can compare original vs enriched metadata, review quality issues, and copy the CDN URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking an AssetCard calls GET /api/assets/:id and opens AssetDetailModal with the response
Modal is a full-screen overlay (fixed, z-index high) with a close button (×) top-right and ESC key listener
Left panel: large image (web_image_path if available, else thumbnail, else grey placeholder), filename, dimensions (W × H px), file size (KB/MB), format
Right panel: metadata table with two columns — 'Original' and 'Enriched'
Metadata rows: Title, Description, Tags (comma-separated), Location, Creator, Rights Owner, Usage Terms, Channel, Format
Enriched cell: shows enriched value in navy text; if null shows grey '—'
If enriched value differs from original: gold 3px left border on enriched cell
Quality Issues section: each issue in quality_issues array displayed as a coloured badge (red for rights/compliance, amber for missing data)
CDN URL section: full url in <code> block, Copy button — on click calls navigator.clipboard.writeText, button shows 'Copied!' for 2s then reverts; hidden entirely if cdn_url is null
'Enrich with AI' button shown if enrichment_source is null or 'pending' — calls enrichAsset(id), shows spinner, refreshes modal data on success
Vitest test: modal renders display_title from mocked asset
Vitest test: Copy button text changes to 'Copied!' after click
Vitest test: ESC keydown event closes the modal (onClose called)
Vitest test: 'Enrich with AI' button is visible when enrichment_source is null
Negative case: cdn_url is null → CDN section not rendered
Negative case: enrichAsset returns 503 → error toast shown, modal stays open

stories.title: Upload component with AI enrichment flow
stories.description: As a content author, I want to drag-and-drop or browse for images and have them automatically enriched with AI metadata so new assets are immediately usable.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the Header 'Upload' button opens UploadModal overlay
UploadModal contains a drag-and-drop zone styled with dashed border: 'Drop images here or click to browse'
Hidden <input type='file' accept='image/jpeg,image/png,image/webp' multiple> triggered on zone click
Toggle button 'Upload folder' switches input to use webkitdirectory attribute for folder selection
After file selection, a file list appears: each row shows filename, file size, and status badge
Client-side validation before upload: files >20MB show status 'Too large (max 20MB)' in red and are excluded from upload
Client-side validation: non-image files show status 'Unsupported type' and are excluded
Submit button 'Upload X files' disabled if no valid files selected
On submit: calls uploadFiles(formData) with all valid files, each file status → 'Uploading...'
On upload success: for each returned asset id, calls enrichAsset(id), file status → 'Enriching...'
On enrichment success: file status → green 'Done ✓'
When all files complete: 'View in library' button appears — clicking it closes modal and calls getAssets() to refresh the grid
If OPENAI_API_KEY not configured: enrichment returns 503 → file status shows amber 'Uploaded — enrichment unavailable'
Vitest test: adding a JPEG file renders it in the file list with 'pending' status
Vitest test: a file >20MB shows 'Too large' status and is not included in formData
Vitest test: submit button disabled when file list is empty
Vitest test: after mocked upload + enrich success, file shows 'Done' status
Negative case: upload API returns 500 → file status 'Upload failed', other files continue
